revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{神社 |名称 = 椿神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=大田区}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[大田区]][[蒲田]]2-20-11 |地図 = |祭神 =…」
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 椿神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=大田区}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[大田区]][[蒲田]]2-20-11 |地図 = |祭神 = [[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]] |社格 = |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''椿神社'''（つばきじんじゃ）は[[東京都]][[大田区]]の[[神社]]。
== 概要 == 創建年代は不明である。地元の人は今でも「どうそじんさま」と呼んでいることから、元々は[[道祖神]]であったと推測される<ref name="史跡散歩">新倉善之 著『大田区史跡散歩 (東京史跡ガイド11)』[[学生社]]、1992年、82-84p</ref>。
当社に伝わる習俗に、麻紐を首に巻くというものがある。[[百日咳]]や[[気管支喘息|喘息]]など[[咳嗽|咳]]の症状があるとき、額堂に掛かっている麻紐を取って首に巻く。治癒した場合は新しい麻紐を奉納する。その際に[[線香]]を供えるなど[[神仏習合|神仏混淆]]の特異な習俗が現在まで残っている<ref name="史跡散歩"></ref>。
これらの習俗と相まって、現在でも地元住民の崇敬の念は高く、[[1987年]]（[[昭和]]62年）に社殿が新築されている<ref name="八幡神社">[https://www.kamatahachiman.org/%E3%83%88%E3%83%83%E3%83%97%E3%83%9A%E3%83%BC%E3%82%B8/%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%E3%81%AE%E5%BE%A1%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85/%E6%A4%BF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 椿神社]蒲田八幡神社</ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[梅屋敷駅 (東京都)|梅屋敷駅]]より徒歩7分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *新倉善之 著『大田区史跡散歩 (東京史跡ガイド11)』[[学生社]]、1992年
== 関連項目 == * [[道祖神]]
==外部リンク== *[https://www.kamatahachiman.org/%E3%83%88%E3%83%83%E3%83%97%E3%83%9A%E3%83%BC%E3%82%B8/%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%E3%81%AE%E5%BE%A1%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85/%E6%A4%BF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 椿神社（蒲田八幡神社サイト内）]
{{Pref-stub|pref=東京都}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つはきしんしや おおたく}} [[Category:東京都区部の神社]] [[Category:大田区の歴史]] [[Category:大田区の建築物]]

{{神社 |名称 = 椿神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=大田区}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[大田区]][[蒲田]]2-20-11 |地図 = |祭神 = [[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]] |社格 = |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''椿神社'''（つばきじんじゃ）は[[東京都]][[大田区]]の[[神社]]。
== 概要 == 創建年代は不明である。地元の人は今でも「どうそじんさま」と呼んでいることから、元々は[[道祖神]]であったと推測される<ref name="史跡散歩">新倉善之 著『大田区史跡散歩 (東京史跡ガイド11)』[[学生社]]、1992年、82-84p</ref>。
当社に伝わる習俗に、麻紐を首に巻くというものがある。[[百日咳]]や[[気管支喘息|喘息]]など[[咳嗽|咳]]の症状があるとき、額堂に掛かっている麻紐を取って首に巻く。治癒した場合は新しい麻紐を奉納する。その際に[[線香]]を供えるなど[[神仏習合|神仏混淆]]の特異な習俗が現在まで残っている<ref name="史跡散歩"></ref>。
これらの習俗と相まって、現在でも地元住民の崇敬の念は高く、[[1987年]]（[[昭和]]62年）に社殿が新築されている<ref name="八幡神社">[https://www.kamatahachiman.org/%E3%83%88%E3%83%83%E3%83%97%E3%83%9A%E3%83%BC%E3%82%B8/%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%E3%81%AE%E5%BE%A1%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85/%E6%A4%BF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 椿神社]蒲田八幡神社</ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[梅屋敷駅 (東京都)|梅屋敷駅]]より徒歩7分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *新倉善之 著『大田区史跡散歩 (東京史跡ガイド11)』[[学生社]]、1992年
== 関連項目 == * [[道祖神]]

{{Pref-stub|pref=東京都}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つはきしんしや おおたく}} [[Category:大田区の神社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 椿神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=大田区}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[大田区]][[蒲田]]2-20-11 |地図 = |祭神 = [[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]] |社格 = |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''椿神社'''（つばきじんじゃ）は[[東京都]][[大田区]]の[[神社]]。[[宗教法人]]格はないが、本務社の[[蒲田八幡神社]]では兼務社扱いとなっている。
== 概要 == 創建年代は不明である。地元の人は今でも「どうそじんさま」と呼んでいることから、元々は[[道祖神]]であったと推測される<ref name="史跡散歩">新倉善之 著『大田区史跡散歩 (東京史跡ガイド11)』[[学生社]]、1992年、82-84p</ref>。
当社に伝わる習俗に、麻紐を首に巻くというものがある。[[百日咳]]や[[気管支喘息|喘息]]など[[咳嗽|咳]]の症状があるとき、額堂に掛かっている麻紐を取って首に巻く。治癒した場合は新しい麻紐を奉納する。その際に[[線香]]を供えるなど[[神仏習合|神仏混淆]]の特異な習俗が現在まで残っている<ref name="史跡散歩"></ref>。
これらの習俗と相まって、現在でも地元住民の崇敬の念は高く、[[1987年]]（[[昭和]]62年）に社殿が新築されている<ref name="八幡神社">[https://www.kamatahachiman.org/%E3%83%88%E3%83%83%E3%83%97%E3%83%9A%E3%83%BC%E3%82%B8/%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%E3%81%AE%E5%BE%A1%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85/%E6%A4%BF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 椿神社]蒲田八幡神社</ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[梅屋敷駅 (東京都)|梅屋敷駅]]より徒歩7分。
== 兼務社 == * [[蒲田八幡神社]]（本務社） * [[薭田神社]] * [[御園神社]] * [[女塚神社]] * [[北野神社 (大田区南蒲田)|北野神社]]
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *新倉善之 著『大田区史跡散歩 (東京史跡ガイド11)』[[学生社]]、1992年
== 関連項目 == * [[道祖神]]
==外部リンク== *[https://www.kamatahachiman.org/%E3%83%88%E3%83%83%E3%83%97%E3%83%9A%E3%83%BC%E3%82%B8/%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%E3%81%AE%E5%BE%A1%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85/%E6%A4%BF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 椿神社（蒲田八幡神社サイト内）]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 椿神社 |画像=[[File:Tsubaki Jinja (Ota, Tokyo).JPG|250px]] |所在地 = [[東京都]][[大田区]][[蒲田]]2-20-11 | 緯度度 = 35| 緯度分 = 33| 緯度秒 = 53.9 | 経度度 = 139| 経度分 = 43| 経度秒 = 31.4 |祭神 = [[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]] |社格 = |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = Tokyo city }} '''椿神社'''（つばきじんじゃ）は[[東京都]][[大田区]]の[[神社]]。[[宗教法人]]格はないが、本務社の[[蒲田八幡神社]]では兼務社扱いとなっている。
== 概要 == 創建年代は不明である。地元の人は今でも「どうそじんさま」と呼んでいることから、元々は[[道祖神]]であったと推測される<ref name="史跡散歩">新倉善之 著『大田区史跡散歩 (東京史跡ガイド11)』[[学生社]]、1992年、82-84p</ref>。
当社に伝わる習俗に、麻紐を首に巻くというものがある。[[百日咳]]や[[気管支喘息|喘息]]など[[咳嗽|咳]]の症状があるとき、額堂に掛かっている麻紐を取って首に巻く。治癒した場合は新しい麻紐を奉納する。その際に[[線香]]を供えるなど[[神仏習合|神仏混淆]]の特異な習俗が現在まで残っている<ref name="史跡散歩"></ref>。
これらの習俗と相まって、現在でも地元住民の崇敬の念は高く、[[1987年]]（[[昭和]]62年）に社殿が新築されている<ref name="八幡神社">[https://www.kamatahachiman.org/%E3%83%88%E3%83%83%E3%83%97%E3%83%9A%E3%83%BC%E3%82%B8/%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%E3%81%AE%E5%BE%A1%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85/%E6%A4%BF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 椿神社]蒲田八幡神社</ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[梅屋敷駅 (東京都)|梅屋敷駅]]より徒歩7分。
== 兼務社 == * [[蒲田八幡神社]]（本務社） * [[薭田神社]] * [[御園神社]] * [[女塚神社]] * [[北野神社 (大田区南蒲田)|北野神社]]
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *新倉善之 著『大田区史跡散歩 (東京史跡ガイド11)』[[学生社]]、1992年
== 関連項目 == * [[道祖神]]
==外部リンク== *[https://www.kamatahachiman.org/%E3%83%88%E3%83%83%E3%83%97%E3%83%9A%E3%83%BC%E3%82%B8/%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%E3%81%AE%E5%BE%A1%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85/%E6%A4%BF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 椿神社（蒲田八幡神社サイト内）]
{{Pref-stub|pref=東京都}} {{shinto-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:つはきしんしや}} [[Category:大田区の神社]]

== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

'''Tsubaki Shrine''' (椿神社, Tsubaki Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōta Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. Though it does not have formal religious corporation status, it is treated as an auxiliary shrine of {{ill|Kamata Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲田八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamata Hachiman Shrine|qq=|1=Kamata Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865214}}, which serves as its supervising shrine.

== Overview ==
The foundation date of the shrine is unknown. It is locally referred to as "Dōsojin-sama," suggesting it originally served as a [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]] (traveler's guardian deity).<ref name="Historic Walk">{{Cite book|author=Yoshiyuki Niikura|title=Ōta Ward Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 11)|publisher=Gakuseisha|year=1992|pages=82-84}}</ref>

One unique custom associated with the shrine involves wrapping a hemp cord around the neck. When someone suffers from symptoms such as [[:en:Whooping cough|whooping cough]] or [[:en:Asthma|asthma]], they take a hemp cord hanging in the shrine's hall and tie it around their neck. If they recover, they offer a new hemp cord to the shrine, often alongside incense offerings. This custom reflects the shrine's blend of [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] (syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism), which persists to this day.<ref name="Historic Walk" />

The shrine remains a focal point of local devotion, and in [[:en:1987|1987]] (Shōwa 62), its main building was reconstructed.<ref name="Hachiman Shrine">{{Cite web|url=https://www.kamatahachiman.org/%E3%83%88%E3%83%83%E3%83%97%E3%83%9A%E3%83%BC%E3%82%B8/%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%E3%81%AE%E5%BE%A1%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85/%E6%A4%BF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Tsubaki Shrine|publisher=Kamata Hachiman Shrine|accessdate=2023-08-04}}</ref>

== Access ==
* A 7-minute walk from [[:en:Umeyashiki Station (Tokyo)|Umeyashiki Station]].

== Auxiliary Shrines ==
The following shrines are supervised by {{ill|Kamata Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲田八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamata Hachiman Shrine|qq=|1=Kamata Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865214}}:
* {{ill|Kamata Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲田八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamata Hachiman Shrine|qq=|1=Kamata Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865214}} (main shrine)
* {{ill|Hibita Shrine|ja|薭田神社|1=Hieta Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibita Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hieta Shrine|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11596288}}
* {{ill|Misono Shrine|ja|御園神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misono Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Onna-zuka Shrine|ja|女塚神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onna-zuka Shrine|qq=|1=Onazuka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97195862}}
* {{ill|Kitano Shrine (Ōta Ward Minami-Kamata)|ja|北野神社 (大田区南蒲田)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Shrine (Ōta Ward Minami-Kamata)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== Further Reading ==

== See Also ==

'''Tsubaki Shrine''' (椿神社, Tsubaki Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōta Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. Though it does not have formal religious corporation status, it is treated as an auxiliary shrine of {{ill|Kamata Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲田八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamata Hachiman Shrine|1=Kamata Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865214}}, which serves as its supervising shrine.

== Overview ==
The foundation date of the shrine is unknown. It is locally referred to as "Dōsojin-sama," suggesting it originally served as a [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]] (traveler's guardian deity).<ref name="Historic Walk">{{Cite book|author=Yoshiyuki Niikura|title=Ōta Ward Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 11)|publisher=Gakuseisha|year=1992|pages=82-84}}</ref>

One unique custom associated with the shrine involves wrapping a hemp cord around the neck. When someone suffers from symptoms such as [[:en:Whooping cough|whooping cough]] or [[:en:Asthma|asthma]], they take a hemp cord hanging in the shrine's hall and tie it around their neck. If they recover, they offer a new hemp cord to the shrine, often alongside incense offerings. This custom reflects the shrine's blend of [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] (syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism), which persists to this day.<ref name="Historic Walk" />

The shrine remains a focal point of local devotion, and in [[:en:1987|1987]] (Shōwa 62), its main building was reconstructed.<ref name="Hachiman Shrine">{{Cite web|url=https://www.kamatahachiman.org/%E3%83%88%E3%83%83%E3%83%97%E3%83%9A%E3%83%BC%E3%82%B8/%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%E3%81%AE%E5%BE%A1%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85/%E6%A4%BF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Tsubaki Shrine|publisher=Kamata Hachiman Shrine|accessdate=2023-08-04}}</ref>

== Access ==
* A 7-minute walk from [[:en:Umeyashiki Station (Tokyo)|Umeyashiki Station]].

'''Tsubaki Shrine''' (椿神社, Tsubaki Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōta Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. Though it does not have formal religious corporation status, it is treated as an auxiliary shrine of {{ill|Kamata Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲田八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamata Hachiman Shrine|1=Kamata Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865214}}, which serves as its supervising shrine.

== Overview ==
The foundation date of the shrine is unknown. It is locally referred to as "Dōsojin-sama," suggesting it originally served as a [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]] (traveler's guardian deity).<ref name="Historic Walk">{{Cite book|author=Yoshiyuki Niikura|title=Ōta Ward Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 11)|publisher=Gakuseisha|year=1992|pages=82-84}}</ref>

One unique custom associated with the shrine involves wrapping a hemp cord around the neck. When someone suffers from symptoms such as [[:en:Whooping cough|whooping cough]] or [[:en:Asthma|asthma]], they take a hemp cord hanging in the shrine's hall and tie it around their neck. If they recover, they offer a new hemp cord to the shrine, often alongside incense offerings. This custom reflects the shrine's blend of [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] (syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism), which persists to this day.<ref name="Historic Walk" />

The shrine remains a focal point of local devotion, and in [[:en:1987|1987]] (Shōwa 62), its main building was reconstructed.<ref name="Hachiman Shrine">{{Cite web|url=https://www.kamatahachiman.org/%E3%83%88%E3%83%83%E3%83%97%E3%83%9A%E3%83%BC%E3%82%B8/%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%E3%81%AE%E5%BE%A1%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85/%E6%A4%BF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Tsubaki Shrine|publisher=Kamata Hachiman Shrine|accessdate=2023-08-04}}</ref>

== Access ==
* A 7-minute walk from [[:en:Umeyashiki Station (Tokyo)|Umeyashiki Station]].

The following shrines are supervised by {{ill|Kamata Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲田八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamata Hachiman Shrine|1=Kamata Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865214}}:

'''Tsubaki Shrine''' (椿神社, Tsubaki Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōta Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. Though it does not have formal religious corporation status, it is treated as an auxiliary shrine of {{ill|Kamata Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲田八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamata Hachiman Shrine|1=Kamata Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865214}}, which serves as its supervising shrine.

== Overview ==
The foundation date of the shrine is unknown. It is locally referred to as "Dōsojin-sama," suggesting it originally served as a [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]] (traveler's guardian deity).<ref name="Historic Walk">{{Cite book|author=Yoshiyuki Niikura|title=Ōta Ward Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 11)|publisher=Gakuseisha|year=1992|pages=82-84}}</ref>

One unique custom associated with the shrine involves wrapping a hemp cord around the neck. When someone suffers from symptoms such as [[:en:Whooping cough|whooping cough]] or [[:en:Asthma|asthma]], they take a hemp cord hanging in the shrine's hall and tie it around their neck. If they recover, they offer a new hemp cord to the shrine, often alongside incense offerings. This custom reflects the shrine's blend of [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] (syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism), which persists to this day.<ref name="Historic Walk" />

The shrine remains a focal point of local devotion, and in [[:en:1987|1987]] (Shōwa 62), its main building was reconstructed.<ref name="Hachiman Shrine">{{Cite web|url=https://www.kamatahachiman.org/%E3%83%88%E3%83%83%E3%83%97%E3%83%9A%E3%83%BC%E3%82%B8/%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%E3%81%AE%E5%BE%A1%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85/%E6%A4%BF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Tsubaki Shrine|publisher=Kamata Hachiman Shrine|accessdate=2023-08-04}}</ref>

== Access ==
* A 7-minute walk from [[:en:Umeyashiki Station (Tokyo)|Umeyashiki Station]].

== Auxiliary Shrines ==
The following shrines are supervised by {{ill|Kamata Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲田八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamata Hachiman Shrine|1=Kamata Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865214}}:
* {{ill|Kamata Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲田八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamata Hachiman Shrine|1=Kamata Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865214}} (main shrine)
* {{ill|Hibita Shrine|ja|薭田神社|1=Hieta Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibita Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hieta Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11596288}}
* {{ill|Misono Shrine|ja|御園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misono Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Onna-zuka Shrine|ja|女塚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onna-zuka Shrine|1=Onazuka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97195862}}
* {{ill|Kitano Shrine (Ōta Ward Minami-Kamata)|ja|北野神社 (大田区南蒲田)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Shrine (Ōta Ward Minami-Kamata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

revision.comment: Bot: add interwikis from Wikidata
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:椿神社 (大田区)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:椿神社 (大田区)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:椿神社 (大田区)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q97196664]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tsubaki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tsubaki Jinja (Ota, Tokyo).JPG | location = 2-20-11 Kamata, [[Ōta Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] | coordinates = {{Coord|35.564972222222224|N|139.7253888888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank = | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall = | alternate_name = | Annual festival = | Rituals = | Map = Tokyo city | location = {{ill|Ota|WD=Q217234}} }} '''Tsubaki Shrine''' (椿神社, Tsubaki Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōta Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. Though it does not have formal religious corporation status, it is treated as an auxiliary shrine of {{ill|Kamata Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲田八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamata Hachiman Shrine|1=Kamata Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865214}}, which serves as its supervising shrine.
== Overview == The foundation date of the shrine is unknown. It is locally referred to as "Dōsojin-sama," suggesting it originally served as a [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]] (traveler's guardian deity).<ref name="Historic Walk">{{Cite book|author=Yoshiyuki Niikura|title=Ōta Ward Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 11)|publisher=Gakuseisha|year=1992|pages=82-84}}</ref>
One unique custom associated with the shrine involves wrapping a hemp cord around the neck. When someone suffers from symptoms such as [[:en:Whooping cough|whooping cough]] or [[:en:Asthma|asthma]], they take a hemp cord hanging in the shrine's hall and tie it around their neck. If they recover, they offer a new hemp cord to the shrine, often alongside incense offerings. This custom reflects the shrine's blend of [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] (syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism), which persists to this day.<ref name="Historic Walk" />
The shrine remains a focal point of local devotion, and in [[:en:1987|1987]] (Shōwa 62), its main building was reconstructed.<ref name="Hachiman Shrine">{{Cite web|url=https://www.kamatahachiman.org/%E3%83%88%E3%83%83%E3%83%97%E3%83%9A%E3%83%BC%E3%82%B8/%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%E3%81%AE%E5%BE%A1%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85/%E6%A4%BF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Tsubaki Shrine|publisher=Kamata Hachiman Shrine|accessdate=2023-08-04}}</ref>
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from [[:en:Umeyashiki Station (Tokyo)|Umeyashiki Station]].
== Auxiliary Shrines == The following shrines are supervised by {{ill|Kamata Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲田八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamata Hachiman Shrine|1=Kamata Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865214}}: * {{ill|Kamata Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲田八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamata Hachiman Shrine|1=Kamata Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865214}} (main shrine) * {{ill|Hibita Shrine|ja|薭田神社|1=Hieta Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibita Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hieta Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11596288}} * {{ill|Misono Shrine|ja|御園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misono Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Onna-zuka Shrine|ja|女塚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onna-zuka Shrine|1=Onazuka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97195862}} * {{ill|Kitano Shrine (Ōta Ward Minami-Kamata)|ja|北野神社 (大田区南蒲田)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Shrine (Ōta Ward Minami-Kamata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:椿神社 (大田区)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:椿神社 (大田区)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:椿神社 (大田区)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q97196664]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tsubaki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tsubaki Jinja (Ota, Tokyo).JPG | location = 2-20-11 Kamata, [[Ōta Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] | coordinates = {{Coord|35.564972222222224|N|139.7253888888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank = | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall = | alternate_name = | Annual festival = | Rituals = | Map = Tokyo city | location = {{ill|Ota|WD=Q217234}} }} '''Tsubaki Shrine''' (椿神社, Tsubaki Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōta Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. Though it does not have formal religious corporation status, it is treated as an auxiliary shrine of {{ill|Kamata Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲田八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamata Hachiman Shrine|1=Kamata Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865214}}, which serves as its supervising shrine.
== Overview == The foundation date of the shrine is unknown. It is locally referred to as "Dōsojin-sama," suggesting it originally served as a [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]] (traveler's guardian deity).<ref name="Historic Walk">{{Cite book|author=Yoshiyuki Niikura|title=Ōta Ward Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 11)|publisher=Gakuseisha|year=1992|pages=82-84}}</ref>
One unique custom associated with the shrine involves wrapping a hemp cord around the neck. When someone suffers from symptoms such as [[:en:Whooping cough|whooping cough]] or [[:en:Asthma|asthma]], they take a hemp cord hanging in the shrine's hall and tie it around their neck. If they recover, they offer a new hemp cord to the shrine, often alongside incense offerings. This custom reflects the shrine's blend of [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] (syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism), which persists to this day.<ref name="Historic Walk" />
The shrine remains a focal point of local devotion, and in [[:en:1987|1987]] (Shōwa 62), its main building was reconstructed.<ref name="Hachiman Shrine">{{Cite web|url=https://www.kamatahachiman.org/%E3%83%88%E3%83%83%E3%83%97%E3%83%9A%E3%83%BC%E3%82%B8/%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%E3%81%AE%E5%BE%A1%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85/%E6%A4%BF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Tsubaki Shrine|publisher=Kamata Hachiman Shrine|accessdate=2023-08-04}}</ref>
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from [[:en:Umeyashiki Station (Tokyo)|Umeyashiki Station]].
== Auxiliary Shrines == The following shrines are supervised by {{ill|Kamata Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲田八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamata Hachiman Shrine|1=Kamata Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865214}}: * {{ill|Kamata Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲田八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamata Hachiman Shrine|1=Kamata Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865214}} (main shrine) * {{ill|Hibita Shrine|ja|薭田神社|1=Hieta Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibita Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hieta Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11596288}} * {{ill|Misono Shrine|ja|御園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misono Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Onna-zuka Shrine|ja|女塚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onna-zuka Shrine|1=Onazuka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97195862}} * {{ill|Kitano Shrine (Ōta Ward Minami-Kamata)|ja|北野神社 (大田区南蒲田)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Shrine (Ōta Ward Minami-Kamata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Yoshiyuki Niikura, *Ōta Ward Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 11)*, Gakuseisha, 1992. == See Also == * [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]]
== External Links == * [https://www.kamatahachiman.org/%E3%83%88%E3%83%83%E3%83%97%E3%83%9A%E3%83%BC%E3%82%B8/%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%E3%81%AE%E5%BE%A1%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85/%E6%A4%BF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsubaki Shrine (within Kamata Hachiman Shrine website)]

When someone suffers from symptoms such as {{ill|Whooping cough|en|Whooping cough|WD=Q134859|lt=whooping cough}} or {{ill|Asthma|en|Asthma|WD=Q35869|lt=asthma}}, they take a hemp cord hanging in the shrine's hall and tie it around their neck. If they recover, they offer a new hemp cord to the shrine, often alongside incense offerings. This custom reflects the shrine's blend of {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} (syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism), which persists to this day.<ref name="Historic Walk" />
The shrine remains a focal point of local devotion, and in {{ill|1987|en|1987|WD=Q2429|lt=1987}} (Shōwa 62), its main building was reconstructed.<ref name="Hachiman Shrine">{{Cite web|url=https://www.kamatahachiman.org/%E3%83%88%E3%83%83%E3%83%97%E3%83%9A%E3%83%BC%E3%82%B8/%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%E3%81%AE%E5%BE%A1%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85/%E6%A4%BF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Tsubaki Shrine|publisher=Kamata Hachiman Shrine|accessdate=2023-08-04}}</ref>

'''Tsubaki Shrine''' (椿神社, Tsubaki Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Ōta Ward]], {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}. Though it does not have formal religious corporation status, it is treated as an auxiliary shrine of {{ill|Kamata Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲田八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamata Hachiman Shrine|1=Kamata Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865214}}, which serves as its supervising shrine.

== Overview ==
The foundation date of the shrine is unknown. It is locally referred to as "Dōsojin-sama," suggesting it originally served as a {{ill|Dōsojin|en|Dōsojin|WD=Q269515|lt=Dōsojin}} (traveler's guardian deity).<ref name="Historic Walk">{{Cite book|author=Yoshiyuki Niikura|title=Ōta Ward Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 11)|publisher=Gakuseisha|year=1992|pages=82-84}}</ref>

One unique custom associated with the shrine involves wrapping a hemp cord around the neck. When someone suffers from symptoms such as {{ill|Whooping cough|en|Whooping cough|WD=Q134859|lt=whooping cough}} or {{ill|Asthma|en|Asthma|WD=Q35869|lt=asthma}}, they take a hemp cord hanging in the shrine's hall and tie it around their neck. If they recover, they offer a new hemp cord to the shrine, often alongside incense offerings. This custom reflects the shrine's blend of {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} (syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism), which persists to this day.<ref name="Historic Walk" />

The shrine remains a focal point of local devotion, and in {{ill|1987|en|1987|WD=Q2429|lt=1987}} (Shōwa 62), its main building was reconstructed.<ref name="Hachiman Shrine">{{Cite web|url=https://www.kamatahachiman.org/%E3%83%88%E3%83%83%E3%83%97%E3%83%9A%E3%83%BC%E3%82%B8/%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%E3%81%AE%E5%BE%A1%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85/%E6%A4%BF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Tsubaki Shrine|publisher=Kamata Hachiman Shrine|accessdate=2023-08-04}}</ref>

== Access ==
* A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Umeyashiki Station (Tokyo)|en|Umeyashiki Station (Tokyo)|WD=Q865643|lt=Umeyashiki Station}}.

== Auxiliary Shrines ==
The following shrines are supervised by {{ill|Kamata Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲田八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamata Hachiman Shrine|1=Kamata Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865214}}:
* {{ill|Kamata Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲田八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamata Hachiman Shrine|1=Kamata Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865214}} (main shrine)
* {{ill|Hibita Shrine|ja|薭田神社|1=Hieta Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibita Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hieta Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11596288}}
* {{ill|Misono Shrine|ja|御園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misono Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Onna-zuka Shrine|ja|女塚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onna-zuka Shrine|1=Onazuka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97195862}}
* {{ill|Kitano Shrine (Ōta Ward Minami-Kamata)|ja|北野神社 (大田区南蒲田)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Shrine (Ōta Ward Minami-Kamata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

== Further Reading ==
* Yoshiyuki Niikura, *Ōta Ward Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 11)*, Gakuseisha, 1992.

This custom reflects the shrine's blend of {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} (syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism), which persists to this day.<ref name="Historic Walk" />
The shrine remains a focal point of local devotion, and in {{ill|1987|en|1987|WD=Q2429|lt=1987}} (Shōwa 62), its main building was reconstructed.<ref name="Hachiman Shrine">{{Cite web|url=https://www.kamatahachiman.org/%E3%83%88%E3%83%83%E3%83%97%E3%83%9A%E3%83%BC%E3%82%B8/%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%E3%81%AE%E5%BE%A1%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85/%E6%A4%BF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Tsubaki Shrine|publisher=Kamata Hachiman Shrine|accessdate=2023-08-04}}</ref>
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Umeyashiki Station (Tokyo)|en|Umeyashiki Station (Tokyo)|WD=Q865643|lt=Umeyashiki Station}}.
== Auxiliary Shrines == The following shrines are supervised by {{ill|Kamata Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲田八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamata Hachiman Shrine|1=Kamata Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865214}}: * {{ill|Kamata Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲田八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamata Hachiman Shrine|1=Kamata Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865214}} (main shrine) * {{ill|Hibita Shrine|ja|薭田神社|1=Hieta Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibita Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hieta Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11596288}} * {{ill|Misono Shrine|ja|御園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misono Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Onna-zuka Shrine|ja|女塚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onna-zuka Shrine|1=Onazuka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97195862}} * {{ill|Kitano Shrine (Ōta Ward Minami-Kamata)|ja|北野神社 (大田区南蒲田)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Shrine (Ōta Ward Minami-Kamata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

'''Tsubaki Shrine''' (椿神社, Tsubaki Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Ōta Ward]], {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}. Though it does not have formal religious corporation status, it is treated as an auxiliary shrine of {{ill|Kamata Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲田八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamata Hachiman Shrine|1=Kamata Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865214}}, which serves as its supervising shrine.

== Overview ==
The foundation date of the shrine is unknown. It is locally referred to as "Dōsojin-sama," suggesting it originally served as a {{ill|Dōsojin|en|Dōsojin|WD=Q269515|lt=Dōsojin|1=Dōsojin}} (traveler's guardian deity).<ref name="Historic Walk">{{Cite book|author=Yoshiyuki Niikura|title=Ōta Ward Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 11)|publisher=Gakuseisha|year=1992|pages=82-84}}</ref>

One unique custom associated with the shrine involves wrapping a hemp cord around the neck. When someone suffers from symptoms such as {{ill|Whooping cough|en|Whooping cough|WD=Q134859|lt=whooping cough|1=Whooping cough}} or {{ill|Asthma|en|Asthma|WD=Q35869|lt=asthma|1=Asthma}}, they take a hemp cord hanging in the shrine's hall and tie it around their neck. If they recover, they offer a new hemp cord to the shrine, often alongside incense offerings. This custom reflects the shrine's blend of {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} (syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism), which persists to this day.<ref name="Historic Walk" />

The shrine remains a focal point of local devotion, and in {{ill|1987|en|1987|WD=Q2429|lt=1987|1=1987}} (Shōwa 62), its main building was reconstructed.<ref name="Hachiman Shrine">{{Cite web|url=https://www.kamatahachiman.org/%E3%83%88%E3%83%83%E3%83%97%E3%83%9A%E3%83%BC%E3%82%B8/%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%E3%81%AE%E5%BE%A1%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85/%E6%A4%BF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Tsubaki Shrine|publisher=Kamata Hachiman Shrine|accessdate=2023-08-04}}</ref>

== Access ==
* A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Umeyashiki Station (Tokyo)|en|Umeyashiki Station (Tokyo)|WD=Q865643|lt=Umeyashiki Station|1=Umeyashiki Station (Tokyo)}}.

== Auxiliary Shrines ==
The following shrines are supervised by {{ill|Kamata Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲田八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamata Hachiman Shrine|1=Kamata Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865214}}:
* {{ill|Kamata Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲田八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamata Hachiman Shrine|1=Kamata Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865214}} (main shrine)
* {{ill|Hibita Shrine|ja|薭田神社|1=Hieta Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibita Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hieta Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11596288}}
* {{ill|Misono Shrine|ja|御園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misono Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Onna-zuka Shrine|ja|女塚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onna-zuka Shrine|1=Onazuka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97195862}}
* {{ill|Kitano Shrine (Ōta Ward Minami-Kamata)|ja|北野神社 (大田区南蒲田)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Shrine (Ōta Ward Minami-Kamata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
